name: From Barco de Viesca to Castro Urdiales - round trip through the Ría de Oriñón
name_original: De Barco de Viesca a Castro Urdiales – ida y vuelta por la Ría de Oriñón
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Liendo offer a diverse landscape for riders. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Cantabrian Sea, featuring coastal roads and estuaries, alongside an interior marked by significant elevation changes. Riders can expect a mix of flat sections near the coast and challenging climbs into the hills, providing varied Liendo cycling terrain and difficulty. The area's topography includes river valleys and higher ground, making it suitable for different levels of road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Liendo The most popular road cycling route is From Barco de Viesca to Castro Urdiales - round trip through the Ría de Oriñón, a 29.8 miles (48.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route features coastal views and passes through the Ría de Oriñón. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ramales de la Victoria – Arredondo, Capital of the World loop from Liendo, a difficult 61.8 miles (99.5 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs and covers a significant distance, offering a challenging experience. Local road cyclists also love the View from Peña Cabarga – Alto de La Encina loop from Liendo, a 60.7 miles (97.7 km) trail leading through elevated terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. This difficult route provides extensive views from its high points. Road cycling around Liendo is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and significant hills, offering a variety of Liendo cycling terrain. The network provides options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to advanced routes with considerable elevation gain. The routes in Liendo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 48 reviews. More than 3,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Liendo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Liendo?
A: Liendo offers a diverse landscape for road cyclists. You'll find coastal roads and estuaries near the Cantabrian Sea, often featuring flatter sections. Moving inland, the terrain becomes more challenging with significant elevation changes, including river valleys and hills. This mix provides varied cycling experiences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Liendo?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy' in the immediate selection, several 'moderate' routes are available. These offer a good balance for those looking for a less strenuous ride compared to the more difficult climbs. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004578384/from-barco-de-viesca-to-castro-urdiales-round-trip-through-the-ria-de-orinon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Barco de Viesca to Castro Urdiales - round trip through the Ría de Oriñón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option with coastal views.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Liendo?
A: Liendo is well-suited for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge. Many routes feature substantial climbs and significant distances. Examples include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31901970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ramales de la Victoria – Arredondo, Capital of the World loop from Liendo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 100 km with over 1700 meters of elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39545835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peña Cabarga – Alto de La Encina loop from Liendo&lt;/a&gt;, offering extensive views from high points.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Liendo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Liendo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. This includes popular options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31901970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ramales de la Victoria – Arredondo, Capital of the World loop from Liendo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39545835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peña Cabarga – Alto de La Encina loop from Liendo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along road cycling routes in Liendo?
A: Several routes pass by historical sites and natural beauty. You might encounter coastal castles and viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6656175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle and Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4692419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castro Urdiales from the Santa Ana Pier&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/534543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Faro del Caballo&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Liendo?
A: The region's proximity to the Cantabrian Sea generally provides a mild climate. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal for road cycling. Summer can be warm, especially inland, while winter rides are possible but may require checking local weather conditions for rain or stronger winds.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Liendo?
A: For families, routes with less elevation gain and away from heavy traffic are generally preferred. While specific 'easy' family routes are not highlighted, the moderate coastal sections, such as parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004578384/from-barco-de-viesca-to-castro-urdiales-round-trip-through-the-ria-de-orinon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Barco de Viesca to Castro Urdiales - round trip through the Ría de Oriñón&lt;/a&gt;, might be suitable for families with some cycling experience. Always check the route's full profile for distance and elevation before setting out.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Liendo?
A: The road cycling routes in Liendo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 48 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from scenic coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs, and the opportunities to explore the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' road cycling routes in Liendo?
A: While the most popular routes are well-trafficked, exploring some of the slightly less-frequented difficult routes can offer a 'hidden gem' experience with stunning views and fewer riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32127304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ampuero Climb – Summit of Fuente Las Varas loop from Hazas&lt;/a&gt; provides a challenging ride through elevated terrain, often with rewarding solitude and panoramic vistas.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Liendo?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes documented around Liendo, catering to various skill levels from moderate to difficult. This selection provides ample opportunities to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Given the region's popularity with tourists and locals, you will likely find cafes and small eateries in the towns and villages that many routes pass through or near. Coastal towns and larger inland villages are good places to plan refreshment stops.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of road cycling routes in Liendo?
A: In most towns and villages that serve as common starting points for these routes, you can typically find public parking. It's advisable to check specific village parking options or look for designated parking areas, especially in popular coastal spots or near trailheads.
tips: Viewpoint between Oriñon and Liendo on the old road. You have to walk about 200 meters with some unevenness to the viewpoint itself. There are beautiful views of all of Liendo and the mountains in the area. It has two wooden tables in different areas.

Impressive cove framed between two hills at the mouth of the Oriñón estuary. The capricious tides constantly change the landscape and on days with waves you can see surfers enjoying themselves in the water.
Incredible path for pedestrians and cyclists (with one lane for each direction) along the old train route. It is 397 meters long and takes two minutes or less to cycle through.
beautiful harbor and old town, a walk on the pier worth it

The most peculiar thing about this lighthouse is its location, since it is located inside the castle of Santa Ana. Outside there are a lot of seagulls and you can see impressive views, even the Basque Country from afar. Highly recommended.

waypoints.name: View of Castro Urdiales from the Santa Ana Pier
